meta.edition: live · Thu July 9 evening · daily cut
meta.note: Fresh day cut. Current events + WC QF night. Comedy craft. Combat silent; civilian angles ok.
comedyRules: never explain the joke; no TED closer
smirk test: if it only sounds smart, rewrite

signals.trend: France–Morocco rematch night: same movie, new budget
signals.whatItIs: World Cup quarterfinal: France vs Morocco is a rematch of the 2022 semi where France ended Morocco's fairytale run. Four years later Morocco has beaten Spain, Portugal, Netherlands, Canada — fans say this time feels different.
signals.whyTrending: Match night. Rematch narrative owns the timeline.
signals.take: same movie, different sequel budget. Morocco brought receipts. France brought the previous ending.
signals.replyTake: "this time feels different" is the most expensive sentence in sports.
signals.codexTake: sequels are dangerous. everybody remembers the ending, nobody remembers the writers changed.

hunt.query: (France OR Morocco) (rematch OR quarter OR World Cup) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: match-night rooms — one line, not a score prediction.

rooms.text: France vs Morocco isn't just a quarterfinal, it's a rematch of the 2022 semifinal… This time feels different.
rooms.replyTake: "this time feels different" is what every rematch says right before it feels exactly the same or violently not.
rooms.codexTake: sports fans say "this time feels different" like the stadium installed new laws of physics.

rooms.text: Morocco will want revenge for the 2022 World Cup semi-final and four years on, they are a much better team.
rooms.replyTake: revenge is just a scouting report with feelings.
rooms.codexTake: revenge is just nostalgia wearing shin guards.

signals.trend: Haaland has scored with everything except the referee's whistle
signals.whatItIs: Erling Haaland has seven goals in four World Cup matches so far — left foot, right foot, header, inside box, outside box. The stat posts are pure flex.
signals.whyTrending: Norway's run + meme that he is a video game character.
signals.take: seven goals, every body part except the VAR monitor. the man is a downloadable content pack.
signals.replyTake: they listed the ways he scores like a product feature matrix.
signals.codexTake: the stat sheet reads like someone selected every attack button and leaned on the controller.

hunt.query: Haaland (goals OR seven OR 7) World Cup min_faves:30 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: stat flex rooms — one dry line.

rooms.text: Erling Haaland has seven goals in four matches… left foot, right foot, header, inside the box, outside the box. You just can't stop him
rooms.why: feature-list format ready for a joke
rooms.replyTake: that's not a scorers chart. that's a skill tree with microtransactions.
rooms.codexTake: at this point the goalkeeper is just providing customer support.

signals.trend: England defender banned for two — available for the final only
signals.whatItIs: FIFA banned Jarell Quansah for two matches after a red card vs Mexico. He misses the Norway quarterfinal and a potential semi. Only path back is the final.
signals.take: suspended for the hard games, eligible for the parade. football discipline has a sense of humor.
signals.replyTake: miss the quarters and semis, free for the trophy lift. productivity hack.
signals.codexTake: two-match ban, final eligibility. football invented missing the meetings and attending the bonus ceremony.

hunt.query: Quansah (banned OR suspended OR red) (England OR Norway OR FIFA) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: suspension news — dry joke, not pile-on.

rooms.text: FIFA just banned England defender Jarell Quansah for 2 matches… miss the quarterfinal against Norway and, if they make it, the semifinal. His only chance to return is the final.
rooms.replyTake: benched for the hard levels, unlocked for the credits. gaming logic.
rooms.codexTake: locked out of the boss fights, eligible for the cutscene.

rooms.text: OFFICIAL: Jarell Quansah suspended for the next two games… miss quarter-final with Norway and a potential semi-final.
rooms.replyTake: calendar says unavailable. destiny says final only. ambitious scheduling.
rooms.codexTake: that suspension has better tournament planning than most squads.

signals.trend: French ref for England vs Norway — the timeline notices
signals.whatItIs: Clément Turpin (French) assigned to England's quarterfinal against Norway. Fans immediately treat nationality as plot.
signals.whyTrending: Referee assignment posts always start a conspiracy seminar.
signals.take: a French referee for England–Norway is either neutral or a Netflix pitch. no in between.
signals.replyTake: the appointment alone has more narrative than half the group stage.
signals.codexTake: nothing says neutral like giving the timeline a beret and a whistle.

hunt.query: (Turpin OR referee OR ref) (England OR Norway) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: ref discourse — joke the narrative, not the person.

rooms.text: French referee Clement Turpin will referee England's quarter-final against Norway.
rooms.why: dry official line, replies already inventing plots
rooms.replyTake: the appointment dropped and the fanfiction started before kickoff.
rooms.codexTake: ref assignments are horoscopes for people who own three scarves.

signals.trend: Morocco never loses under an Argentinian ref (sample size: yes)
signals.whatItIs: Opta note: Morocco undefeated in World Cup games refereed by Argentinians (W1 D2). Posted with 'Confident.'
signals.whyTrending: Tiny-sample superstition is peak World Cup content.
signals.take: three games is a sample size and a religion.
signals.replyTake: W1 D2 is not a record. it's a horoscope with a logo.
signals.codexTake: W1 D2 is less data than a fortune cookie and somehow twice as confident.

hunt.query: Morocco (Argentinian OR Argentina) (referee OR ref OR lost) min_faves:30 -filter:replies lang:en

rooms.text: 0 - Morocco have never lost a FIFA World Cup game that has been refereed by an Argentinian (W1 D2). Confident.
rooms.replyTake: confident is doing a lot of work for a sample that fits in a group chat.
rooms.codexTake: Opta discovered astrology with possession stats.

signals.trend: anybody with a good conscience doesn't want Argentina to win
signals.whatItIs: Hot-take post claiming people with a conscience don't want Argentina to win the World Cup — pure bait designed for argument.
signals.whyTrending: Moral framing of sports rivalry always clears replies.
signals.take: conscience is doing a lot of work for club allegiances and old grudges.
signals.replyTake: interesting moral framework. does it cover set pieces.
signals.codexTake: morality entering sports discourse like it got lost on the way to church.

hunt.query: Argentina (conscience OR hate OR want) World Cup min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: bait room — one dry deflation, no pile-on.

rooms.text: anybody with a good conscience doesn't want Argentina to win the World Cup.
rooms.replyTake: conscience is a strong word for vibes and a prior tournament.
rooms.codexTake: good conscience is a heavy jersey for a rivalry post.

signals.trend: nearly half of Americans under 30 live with a parent
signals.whatItIs: WSJ-linked stat: nearly half of American adults under 30 live with a parent. Housing, wages, and the 'adulthood timeline' collide.
signals.whyTrending: Affordability discourse with a clean number.
signals.take: adulthood got delayed and the spare bedroom became infrastructure.
signals.replyTake: the starter home is your childhood bedroom with better wifi.
signals.codexTake: the American dream moved back upstairs and pays rent in vibes.

hunt.query: ("under 30" OR "live with" OR parents) (housing OR home OR WSJ) min_faves:40 -filter:replies lang:en

rooms.text: BREAKING: Nearly half of American adults under 30 are living with a parent, per WSJ
rooms.why: mega room — only if the line is sharp and non-partisan
rooms.replyTake: we rebranded the spare bedroom as housing policy and pretended it was culture.
rooms.codexTake: everyone wanted generational housing. surprise, it's bunk beds with LinkedIn.

signals.trend: Arsenal leading Premier League clubs in World Cup goal involvements
signals.whatItIs: Fan/stat post: Arsenal players have the most goal involvements among Premier League clubs at the 2026 World Cup.
signals.take: the club season never ended. it just changed jersey and timezone.
signals.replyTake: international break is just the Premier League with worse commentary rights.
signals.codexTake: club fans can turn an international tournament into a quarterly earnings call.

hunt.query: Arsenal (World Cup) (goals OR involvements OR most) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en

rooms.text: Arsenal are the Premier League Club with the MOST goal involvements in the 2026 World Cup.
rooms.replyTake: the league table followed them through passport control.
rooms.codexTake: Arsenal found a way to top a table that does not actually exist. elite heritage.

signals.trend: White House covered scaffolding to fake the finished columns
signals.whatItIs: Posts claim Trump draped a cover over White House north portico scaffolding to create the illusion of repaired columns while work continues. Optics as renovation.
signals.take: when the renovation is slow, ship a texture pack. government as open world game.
signals.replyTake: they patched the UI. the geometry is still compiling.
signals.codexTake: politics is now a home renovation show where the before picture gets a press secretary.

hunt.query: ("White House" OR scaffolding OR portico) (columns OR cover OR repaired) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: optics joke — not a policy war.

rooms.text: Trump draped a cover over the scaffolding at the White House north portico to create the illusion of repaired columns while the work is being done.
rooms.replyTake: demo day energy. the columns are a loading screen.
rooms.codexTake: load-bearing Photoshop, finally in federal architecture.

signals.trend: score first, cash even if you lose — the book as tragic hero
signals.whatItIs: World Cup betting promo: score first and get paid regardless of final result. Stake CEO estimated $50M+ losses on the promo.
signals.take: they sold insurance on the plot twist and the plot twist is winning.
signals.replyTake: losing $50M on purpose and announcing it is marketing or performance art. both.
signals.codexTake: gambling company discovers risk, issues press release with confetti.

hunt.query: (Stake OR "score first") (World Cup OR $50 OR promotion) min_faves:15 -filter:replies lang:en

rooms.text: Our data team estimates Stake will lose over $50M across the remainder of the World Cup because of this promotion
rooms.replyTake: announcing a $50M loss like a product launch. confidence.
rooms.codexTake: nothing says trust us with money like announcing the money left.

signals.trend: Hormuz still the civilian dashboard for the war
signals.whatItIs: Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains a live metric for US-Iran tension — low ship counts, oil anxiety, repair news on rail links. Civilian economy as scoreboard.
signals.whyTrending: Ongoing. Prefer trade/pump frame over combat.
signals.take: foreign policy is a shipping tracker and a gas receipt.
signals.replyTake: the front line most people can feel is still the pump.
signals.codexTake: global stability has become a tab you refresh while gasoline quietly judges you.

hunt.query: (Hormuz) (ships OR traffic OR oil) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en

rooms.text: critical shipping lane emptying… only 14 cargo ships… chokepoint for ~20% of global oil.
rooms.replyTake: 14 ships is the global economy refreshing a status page.
rooms.codexTake: fourteen ships is not a statistic. it's the economy breathing through a straw.

signals.trend: grocery before noon feels like conquering the world
signals.whatItIs: Small post: getting to the grocery store before noon feels like a life win. Soft comedy of adult logistics.
signals.take: the victory conditions of adulthood keep getting smaller and more true.
signals.replyTake: before noon at the store is the only campaign win some of us will claim this quarter.
signals.codexTake: before-noon grocery shopping is adulthood's moon landing, except the flag is a spinach receipt.

rooms.text: When I get to the grocery store before noon I feel like I have tackled the world
rooms.replyTake: main quest complete. side quest: put the milk away before it dies in the car.
rooms.codexTake: the milk making it home cold is basically NATO logistics.

signals.trend: US–Iran strikes, naval deaths, rail bridges — combat feed
signals.whatItIs: Active conflict reporting: strikes, infrastructure damage/repair, naval commander killed near Hormuz. Not for growth replies.
signals.whyTrending: Hard news. Stay silent. Civilian cost lives in Hormuz/gas cards.

signals.whatItIs: Assorted emergency/tragedy posts still circulating. Not for commentary growth.
